title: Lambda expressions
successMessage: Three lambdas: a Comparator for sort, a Consumer for forEach, a Predicate for removeIf. Lambdas are implementations of functional interfaces — one abstract method.

title: Method references
instruction:
Method references are shorter lambdas when you just call an existing method:

```java
names.forEach(name -> System.out.println(name));  // lambda
names.forEach(System.out::println);               // method reference
```

Four types: `ClassName::staticMethod`, `object::instanceMethod`, `ClassName::instanceMethod`, `ClassName::new`
successMessage: System.out::println, Main::addExclamation, String::toUpperCase — all method references. They're just syntactic sugar for lambdas but read more naturally.

title: Stream basics — filter and map
instruction:
A Stream pipeline: source → intermediate operations → terminal operation.

- `filter(predicate)` — keep matching elements
- `map(function)` — transform each element
- `collect(Collectors.toList())` — produce a List result

Streams are lazy — nothing runs until the terminal operation.
successMessage: filter keeps Alice/Carol/David/Frank (length > 4). map uppercases. count() is a terminal op that returns the count without collecting. Each intermediate op returns a new Stream.

title: reduce, sorted, distinct
instruction:
More stream operations:

- `sorted()` — sort elements
- `distinct()` — remove duplicates
- `reduce(identity, accumulator)` — combine all elements to one value
- `mapToInt()` → `.sum()` / `.average()` / `.max()`
successMessage: reduce(0, Integer::sum) starts at 0 and adds each element. distinct() removes 5, 3, 8 duplicates. summaryStatistics() gives count, sum, min, max, average in one call.

title: Collectors — grouping and joining
instruction:
`Collectors` provides powerful terminal operations:

- `Collectors.toList()` — collect to List
- `Collectors.joining(", ")` — join strings
- `Collectors.groupingBy(f)` — group into Map
- `Collectors.counting()` — count per group
successMessage: groupingBy builds a Map automatically. Collectors.counting() downstream counts per group. This groupingBy + counting combo is a one-liner word frequency counter.

title: Optional — null safety
successMessage: Optional forces you to handle the 'not found' case. orElse provides a default. map transforms if present. This eliminates NullPointerException in stream operations.

title: Build a report generator
instruction:
Final challenge — no starter code.

Given a list of `Employee` objects (name, department, salary):
1. Find the highest earner in each department (groupingBy + Collectors.maxBy)
2. Average salary per department (groupingBy + Collectors.averagingDouble)
3. All employees earning > 80000, sorted by salary desc, collected to List
4. Print a formatted report

Create at least 8 employees in 3 departments.
hint: record Employee(String name, String dept, double salary) {} — use record for brevity (Chapter 22), or a normal class.
successMessage: A full report generator using Streams. groupingBy with maxBy or averagingDouble is the pattern for report systems everywhere. Chapter 17 done!
